description: A plain candid nighttime photograph (no on-image text, slogan, or graphic design) of the facade of a concrete government office building, with a bare-branched tree silhouetted in the foreground at left. Most windows are dark, but two adjacent windows are lit with bright blue/teal lights, displaying two glowing vertical light bars in tribute to Paul Revere's 'one if by land, two if by sea' lanterns ('two lights for tomorrow' / #twolightsfortomorrow) marking the 250th anniversary of his 1775 ride. Because it is simply a documentary photo of the lit DOL headquarters with no added typography or branding, it is not a designed meme or poster.
